text.title: Regulatory Compliance 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the 2026 accessibility-regulation compliance report. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every product surface (web, native app, kiosk, e-book) MUST be mapped to applicable regulations with named jurisdictions: ADA Title II (US public entities, WCAG 2.1 AA, April 2026), EAA (EU, EN 301 549 / WCAG 2.1 AA, June 2025), AODA (Ontario, WCAG 2.0 AA), Section 508 (US federal procurement, WCAG 2.0 AA). The earliest deadline is the constraint.

rationale.source: ADA Title II Final Rule 2024; Directive (EU) 2019/882

rule.rationale: Regulatory applicability varies by jurisdiction and product type; explicit mapping forces decisions about which surfaces fall under which rules and surfaces the binding deadline.

rule.statement: WCAG 2.2 AA is the design baseline; WCAG 2.1 AA is the legal minimum. Documents MUST cite the version and level explicitly; "AA-compliant" without version is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Designing to 2.2 now avoids gap-closing later when regulations advance; un-versioned claims are unenforceable and litigation-exposed.

rule.statement: A dated accessibility statement MUST be published per public-facing surface, naming WCAG version + level, the testing methodology (manual + automated + AT-user), a feedback channel (email + phone + form), and a remediation commitment. Update quarterly or after every major release.

rationale.source: ADA Title II Final Rule §35.200; EAA Annex V

rule.rationale: Courts and regulators read the published statement as the surface's compliance posture; absence or staleness is treated as bad faith.

rule.statement: Conformance claims MUST be backed by documented testing: automated scan logs (axe / pa11y / Lighthouse), manual screen-reader/keyboard records, and at least one assistive-technology user session per major release. Automated-only conformance is forbidden.

rationale.source: WebAIM 2024 — automated tools detect 30-40% of WCAG issues

rule.rationale: Automated tools cannot evaluate context-dependent failures (label clarity, reading order, focus management); claiming conformance without manual + AT evidence creates legal exposure.

rule.statement: Accessibility overlay widgets (AccessiBe, UserWay, audioEye in auto-fix mode) MUST NOT be deployed as a compliance strategy. Disable any inherited overlay before publishing the statement.

rationale.source: 2023-2024 US court rulings — Murphy v. Eyebobs et al.

rule.rationale: Overlays have repeatedly lost in court and frequently break real assistive technology; they create liability rather than reducing it.

rule.statement: Content authors (marketing, support, legal) MUST receive WCAG basics training (alt-text, heading order, link labels, color contrast) and be measured on a11y defects per release. Engineering alone cannot enforce.

rationale.source: WebAIM Million 2024 — >95% of homepages with violations

rule.rationale: Most a11y violations enter through CMS-edited content, not engineering code; training is the lowest-cost gate.
